Name: adaptive_hardware_aware_quantization
Title: Adaptive Hardware-Aware Mixed-Precision Quantization for Real-Time Inference
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging hardware profiling to dynamically adjust mixed-precision quantization at runtime, we can achieve significant improvements in inference speed and memory efficiency without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: Current literature focuses on static mixed-precision quantization approaches or those that require extensive calibration and training (e.g., KVTuner, MxMoE). Our approach distinguishes itself by implementing adaptive quantization that responds in real-time to hardware performance metrics, optimizing for throughput and latency based on the current workload and context.
Abstract: In the realm of machine learning inference, particularly for large language models (LLMs) and vision transformers, the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency is crucial. While existing mixed-precision quantization techniques have shown promise, they often rely on static configurations or extensive pre-calibration, which can lead to suboptimal performance in dynamic environments. This paper proposes a novel adaptive hardware-aware mixed-precision quantization framework that utilizes real-time profiling of GPU performance metrics—such as throughput, latency, and memory utilization—to inform quantization decisions at runtime. By continuously adjusting the bit-width of activations and weights based on live performance data, we aim to enhance inference speed and reduce memory overhead while maintaining model accuracy. Experimental evaluations will demonstrate the framework's ability to outperform existing static approaches, achieving significant speedups and efficiency gains on commodity GPUs under varying workloads.
Experiments: Implement a baseline mixed-precision quantization approach on a selected LLM and ViT architecture, measuring p50/p99 latency and throughput using fixed configurations.
Develop the adaptive quantization framework that profiles GPU performance metrics during inference and dynamically adjusts the precision of layers based on predefined heuristics.
Run the adaptive quantization model against the baseline on various datasets and workloads, capturing performance metrics and analyzing the trade-offs between accuracy, speed, and memory usage.
Evaluate the performance gains in terms of throughput (tokens per second) and latency (in milliseconds) under varying batch sizes and input complexities.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the overhead introduced by real-time profiling, which may negate some performance gains. Additionally, the effectiveness of adaptive strategies may vary across different models and hardware configurations. Limitations may also arise in ensuring the adaptive mechanism does not lead to excessive variability in performance metrics.
